title: agrostis castellana subsp. byzantina
intro: Agrostis castellana subsp. byzantina, also known as 'Maryland sedge', is a type of perennial grass native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and even act as a diuretic. Agrostis castellana subsp. byzantina can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used in traditional herbal remedies for its medicinal effects.
